nodes.description: Week 1: I Do It: Introduce rhyming words with a video. We Do It: Students find rhyming words in pairs. You Do It: Students read a story with rhyming words. You Do It: Present poems to the class.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] European Union's trade policy impacts developing countries positively and negatively
nodes.description: The European Union's trade policy has both positive and negative impacts on developing countries, providing access to its market through trade agreements while also favoring its own industries, making it difficult for developing countries to compete.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] Bangladesh benefited from Everything But Arms (EBA) initiative
nodes.description: Bangladesh has benefited significantly from the Everything But Arms (EBA) initiative, which provides duty-free and quota-free access to the EU market for all products except arms and ammunition, helping its garment industry grow and lift many out of poverty.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] Kenya benefited from EU's Generalized System of Preferences (GSP+) program
nodes.description: Kenya has benefited from the EU's Generalized System of Preferences (GSP+) program, which provides additional trade benefits for countries implementing certain labor and environmental standards, particularly benefiting Kenya's floriculture industry.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] Many African countries harmed by EU's agricultural subsidies
nodes.description: Many African countries have been harmed by the EU's agricultural subsidies, which make it difficult for their farmers to compete, with specific examples including the decline of sugar production in Malawi, Mozambique, and Zambia due to EU's sugar subsidies.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] Cambodia facing partial withdrawal of EBA privileges
nodes.description: Cambodia is facing the partial withdrawal of EBA privileges due to concerns about human rights and labor rights abuses, which may harm its garment and footwear industry that employs around 700,000 people.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] Developing countries can diversify their export basket
nodes.description: Developing countries should pursue diversification of their export basket by promoting exports of goods and services which are less vulnerable to competition in the EU's internal market.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] Developing countries can promote regional economic integration
nodes.description: Developing countries can promote regional economic integration by forming economic blocs or free trade areas to create larger markets, reduce trade costs, and increase coordination to compete effectively.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] Developing countries can strengthen domestic industries
nodes.description: Developing countries can strengthen their domestic industries by investing in human capital, infrastructure development, innovation, and implementing reforms on property rights to increase competitiveness in the global market.

nodes.title: [2023-05-23 23:11] Developing countries should negotiate favorable trade agreements with the EU
nodes.description: Developing countries should negotiate favorable trade agreements with the EU by pushing for reductions in trade barriers and ensuring their inclusion in trade agreements that take into account their particular interests.

nodes.description: Developing countries can seek technical assistance from international organizations such as UNCTAD, the World Bank, and the International Monetary Fund (IMF) to help develop better trade policies and strategies.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines work by introducing a small or weakened form of a pathogen into the body
nodes.description: Vaccines introduce a small or weakened form of a pathogen into the body, allowing the immune system to recognize the pathogen and create antibodies to fight against it.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines are important for global health because they prevent the spread of infectious diseases
nodes.description: Vaccines are one of the most effective ways to prevent the spread of infectious diseases, playing a critical role in eradicating diseases such as smallpox and reducing the incidence of others such as polio and measles.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines help create herd immunity by vaccinating a large percentage of the population
nodes.description: Vaccines help to create herd immunity, meaning that when a large percentage of a population is vaccinated, even those who are not vaccinated are less likely to become infected, thus protecting vulnerable populations.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines may have mild and temporary side effects
nodes.description: Vaccines may have side effects that are generally mild and temporary, such as soreness at the injection site or a mild fever, while serious side effects are extremely rare.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines undergo rigorous testing before being licensed for use
nodes.description: Before a vaccine is licensed, it undergoes rigorous testing including laboratory and animal studies, as well as multiple phases of clinical trials in humans, ensuring safety and effectiveness.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccine safety is continuously monitored after approval
nodes.description: The safety of vaccines is continuously monitored even after they are licensed, with safety data collected through various reporting systems and studies.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines are evaluated for safety and effectiveness individually and in combination
nodes.description: Vaccines are evaluated for safety and effectiveness both individually and in combination with other vaccines to ensure they are safe when given together as part of a recommended schedule.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines prevent serious long-term health effects caused by diseases
nodes.description: There is no evidence to suggest that vaccines cause long-term health problems; in fact, the diseases that vaccines protect against can cause serious long-term health effects, disabilities, and even death.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines are developed by both public and private sectors
nodes.description: Vaccines are developed by both public and private sectors with the goal of protecting public health, and while financial considerations exist, the primary goal is to prevent diseases and save lives.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Pharmaceutical companies must adhere to strict regulatory requirements for vaccine safety
nodes.description: Pharmaceutical companies are held accountable for the safety and efficacy of all drugs and vaccines they produce, adhering to strict regulatory requirements before a vaccine can be licensed.

nodes.title: [2023-05-25 09:31] Vaccines can be expensive but are often provided at no cost or reduced prices
nodes.description: Vaccines can be expensive, particularly in countries where they are not universally provided, but many healthcare systems or public health programs provide vaccines at no cost or at reduced prices for those who cannot afford them.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Photosynthesis occurs in plants, algae, and some bacteria
nodes.description: Photosynthesis is a process that occurs in plants, algae, and some bacteria, in which light energy is converted into chemical energy.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Photosynthesis occurs in chloroplasts of plant cells
nodes.description: This process occurs in the chloroplasts of plant cells, and is essential for the production of food and oxygen in the biosphere.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Light energy is absorbed by pigments such as chlorophyll during photosynthesis
nodes.description: During photosynthesis, light energy is absorbed by pigments such as chlorophyll, and is used to convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Glucose is produced during photosynthesis and used by plants as energy
nodes.description: The glucose is used by the plant as a source of energy, while the oxygen is released into the atmosphere.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Fermentation occurs in the absence of oxygen
nodes.description: Fermentation is a process that occurs in the absence of oxygen, in which organic compounds are broken down into simpler molecules.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Microorganisms such as yeast and bacteria carry out fermentation
nodes.description: This process is carried out by microorganisms such as yeast and bacteria, and is used to produce a variety of products, including alcohol, bread, yogurt, and other fermented foods.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Microorganisms convert sugars into alcohol and carbon dioxide during fermentation
nodes.description: During fermentation, the microorganisms convert sugars and other organic compounds into alcohol, carbon dioxide, and other products.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Alcohol produced during fermentation is used in beverages
nodes.description: The alcohol produced during fermentation is used in the production of beverages such as beer and wine, while the carbon dioxide is used in the production of bread and other fermented foods.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Photosynthesis and fermentation are essential processes for production of various products
nodes.description: Overall, photosynthesis and fermentation are two important processes that are carried out by microorganisms, and are essential for the production of a wide range of products that are used by humans and other organisms.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Heterologous proteins are produced by genetically modified microorganisms
nodes.description: Heterologous proteins are proteins that are produced by a microorganism that is different from the organism in which the protein naturally occurs. This type of protein production is often carried out using genetically modified microorganisms, which have been engineered to express the desired protein.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Transformation is a method to produce heterologous proteins in microbes
nodes.description: Transformation is a process in which a microorganism is genetically modified by introducing new genetic material into its genome. This is often done using plasmids, which are small circular pieces of DNA that can be easily transferred between different organisms.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Transduction is a method to produce heterologous proteins in microbes
nodes.description: Transduction is a process in which a microorganism is genetically modified by introducing new genetic material into its genome through the action of a virus. The virus carries the desired genetic material into the microorganism, where it is incorporated into the genome.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Cloning is a method to produce heterologous proteins in microbes
nodes.description: Cloning is a process in which a specific gene or piece of DNA is copied and inserted into a microorganism, allowing it to express the desired protein. This is often done using a plasmid or other vector, which carries the desired genetic material into the microorganism.

nodes.title: [2023-05-27 09:29] Production of heterologous proteins in microbes is important in biotechnology
nodes.description: Overall, the production of heterologous proteins in microbes is an important tool in biotechnology, as it allows for the production of proteins that may not be readily available from natural sources.
